dealRisks.risk: CEO Nigel Darwin still has zero Shopify engagement — contract signatory with no direct relationship. Risk of last-minute objections or delays at signature.
Test system access not yet provisioned — Mick and Ryan urgently need hands-on access. Delay risks losing momentum from highly positive POS demo.
Hardware demo still not rescheduled — originally cancelled Feb 19. Physical POS device evaluation is a prerequisite for store rollout planning.
Budget sign-off still pending ('any day now' since Feb 19) — 3+ weeks of delay suggests internal friction or competing priorities.

updates.notes: Technical complications now fully surfaced in Mar 9 POS demo: OneStock integration, GiveX gift cards, ERP product data, ~20K styles with variants, multi-currency catalogs. All addressed.
Buying committee well-defined: Andy Grainger (CTO) designated Mick Burfoot as project lead. Full tech team identified: Mick (BSA/lead), Mark (dev), Ryan (BSA), Ian (dev).
Affected teams fully mapped in Mar 9 call: Business systems team articulated specific pain across POS/eCom/integration/permissions. Store ops across 55+ UK/Ireland/outlet stores affected.
CEO not directly engaged but CTO Andy designated project lead, full tech team engaged. Martin bridging to decision structure. Indirect but meaningful progress toward buying group.

newMAPItems.milestone: Test system access provisioned for Mick and Ryan
Product data loaded into test environment (~20K styles)
Hardware demo and POS device evaluation
Budget sign-off confirmed by Nigel Darwin

narrativeAdditions.oppSummary: As of March 9, the deal has progressed from executive-level evaluation to active technical validation. CTO Andy Grainger has formally designated Mick Burfoot as project lead, and a 4-person technical team is now deeply engaged. Martin Wareing is transitioning to executive sponsor role. The team's urgent request for test system access signals transition from evaluation to implementation planning.
narrativeAdditions.whyChange: March 9 POS demo revealed additional pain: Dune stopped selling gift cards after SFCC migration (lost revenue stream), separate refund systems across channels, Anakta POS lacks granular permissions ('we are control freaks'), and ~20K product styles require fragile delta feed architecture.
narrativeAdditions.whyShopify: March 9 POS demo validated fit on critical requirements: single admin for UK/Ireland/outlet with separate currencies and catalogs, native GiveX integration, OneStock as Shopify partner, granular RBAC, and unified returns. Technical team's reaction was constructive with no blockers.
narrativeAdditions.whyNow: Urgency escalated March 9. Mick stated 'we need to crack on' multiple times. Request for immediate test system access signals real deadline pressure. Deal transitioning from decision to implementation planning.
narrativeAdditions.supportNeeded: New from March 9: Immediate test system access for Mick and Ryan. Technical enablement for business systems team. API docs for product data feeds, delta stock updates, ERP integration. GiveX integration guidance. Hardware demo still needs rescheduling.

newCall.title: Dune <> Shopify POS Demo
newCall.summary: Full POS demo covering unified selling, multi-currency/multi-catalog, returns, permissions, integrations (OneStock, GiveX, ERP). Technical team highly engaged, no blockers. Urgent request for test system access.
